result.stdout:
[11:55:32 INF] WEBSYSTEM CLI (https://webSystem.io)
[11:55:33 INF] Version 3.1.0-dev (Development channel)
[11:55:33 INF] 
Usage:

    webSystem <command> <target> [options]

Command List:

    > help: Show command line help. Write ` webSystem help <command> `
    > new: Generate a new solution based on the WEBSYSTEM startup templates.
    > get-source: Download the source code of the specified module.
    > update: Update all WEBSYSTEM related NuGet packages and NPM packages in a solution or project to the latest version.
    > add-package: Add a new WEBSYSTEM package to a project by adding related NuGet package dependencies and [DependsOn(...)] attributes.
    > add-module: Add a multi-package module to a solution by finding all packages of the module, finding related projects in the solution and adding each package to the corresponding project in the solution.
    > login: Sign in to https://account.webSystem.io/.
    > logout: Sign out from https://account.webSystem.io/.
    > generate-proxy: Generates typescript service proxies and DTOs
    > suite: Install, update, remove or start WEBSYSTEM Suite. See https://commercial.webSystem.io/tools/suite.
    > switch-to-preview: Switches packages to preview WEBSYSTEM version.
    > switch-to-stable: Switches packages to stable WEBSYSTEM version from preview version.
    > switch-to-nightly: Switches packages to nightly preview WEBSYSTEM version.
    > translate: Mainly used to translate WEBSYSTEM's resources (JSON files) easier.

To get a detailed help for a command:

    webSystem help <command>

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.webSystem.io/en/webSystem/latest/CLI

result.stdout:
[11:55:34 INF] WEBSYSTEM CLI (https://webSystem.io)
[11:55:34 INF] Version 3.1.0-dev (Development channel)
[11:55:34 WRN] Module name is missing!


'add-module' command is used to add a multi-package WEBSYSTEM module to a solution.
It should be used in a folder containing a .sln file.

Usage:
  webSystem add-module <module-name> [options]

Options:
  --with-source-code                              Downloads the source code of the module and adds it to your solution.
  -s|--solution <solution-file>                   Specify the solution file explicitly.
  --skip-db-migrations <boolean>                  Specify if a new migration will be added or not.
  -sp|--startup-project <startup-project-path>    Relative path to the project folder of the startup project. Default value is the current folder.
  -v|--version <version>                          Specify the version of the module. Default is your project's WEBSYSTEM version.

Examples:

  webSystem add-module Beiyuan.Blogging                      Adds the module to the current solution.
  webSystem add-module Beiyuan.Blogging -s Acme.BookStore    Adds the module to the given solution.
  webSystem add-module Beiyuan.Blogging -s Acme.BookStore --skip-db-migrations false    Adds the module to the given solution but doesn't create a database migration.
  webSystem add-module Beiyuan.Blogging -s Acme.BookStore -sp ..\Acme.BookStore.Web\Acme.BookStore.Web.csproj   Adds the module to the given solution and specify migration startup project.

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.webSystem.io/en/webSystem/latest/CLI

[11:55:34 INF] WEBSYSTEM CLI (https://webSystem.io)
[11:55:34 INF] Version 3.1.0-dev (Development channel)
[11:55:34 WRN] Package name is missing!


'add-package' command is used to add an WEBSYSTEM package to a project.
It should be used in a folder containing a .csproj file.

Usage:

  webSystem add-package <package-name> [options]

Options:

  -p|--project <project-file>    Specify the project file explicitly.
  -v|--version <version>         Specify the version of the package. Default is your project's WEBSYSTEM version or latest WEBSYSTEM version.

Examples:

  webSystem add-package Beiyuan.WebSystem.FluentValidation                                  Adds the package to the current project.
  webSystem add-package Beiyuan.WebSystem.FluentValidation -p Acme.BookStore.Application    Adds the package to the given project.

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.webSystem.io/en/webSystem/latest/CLI

Usage:

Options:

-u|--ui <ui-framework>               (default: angular)

Examples:

result.stdout:
[11:55:33 INF] WEBSYSTEM CLI (https://webSystem.io)
[11:55:33 INF] Version 3.1.0-dev (Development channel)
[11:55:33 WRN] Project name is missing!


Usage:

  webSystem new <project-name> [options]

Options:

-t|--template <template-name>               (default: app)
-u|--ui <ui-framework>                      (if supported by the template)
-m|--mobile <mobile-framework>              (if supported by the template)
-d|--database-provider <database-provider>  (if supported by the template)
-o|--output-folder <output-folder>          (default: current folder)
-v|--version <version>                      (default: latest version)
--preview                                   (Use latest pre-release version if there is at least one pre-release after latest stable version)
-ts|--template-source <template-source>     (your local or network webSystem template source)
-csf|--create-solution-folder               (default: true)
-cs|--connection-string <connection-string> (your database connection string)
--tiered                                    (if supported by the template)
--no-ui                                     (if supported by the template)
--separate-identity-server                  (if supported by the template)
--local-framework-ref --webSystem-path <your-local-webSystem-repo-path>  (keeps local references to projects instead of replacing with NuGet package references)

Examples:

  webSystem new Acme.BookStore
  webSystem new Acme.BookStore --tiered
  webSystem new Acme.BookStore -u angular
  webSystem new Acme.BookStore -u angular -d mongodb
  webSystem new Acme.BookStore -m none
  webSystem new Acme.BookStore -m react-native
  webSystem new Acme.BookStore -d mongodb
  webSystem new Acme.BookStore -d mongodb -o d:\my-project
  webSystem new Acme.BookStore -t module
  webSystem new Acme.BookStore -t module --no-ui
  webSystem new Acme.BookStore -t console
  webSystem new Acme.BookStore -ts "D:\localTemplate\webSystem"
  webSystem new Acme.BookStore -csf false
  webSystem new Acme.BookStore --local-framework-ref --webSystem-path "D:\github\webSystem"
  webSystem new Acme.BookStore --connection-string "Server=myServerName\myInstanceName;Database=myDatabase;User Id=myUsername;Password=myPassword"

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.webSystem.io/en/webSystem/latest/CLI

[11:55:41 INF] WEBSYSTEM CLI (https://webSystem.io)
[11:55:41 INF] Version 3.1.0-dev (Development channel)
[11:55:41 INF] 
Usage:

  webSystem suite [options]

Options:

<no argument>                          (run WEBSYSTEM Suite)
install                                (install WEBSYSTEM Suite as a dotnet global tool)
update                                 (update WEBSYSTEM Suite to the latest)
remove                                 (uninstall WEBSYSTEM Suite)

Examples:

  webSystem suite
  webSystem suite install
  webSystem suite update
  webSystem suite remove

[11:55:45 INF] WEBSYSTEM CLI (https://webSystem.io)
[11:55:45 INF] Version 3.1.0-dev (Development channel)
[11:55:45 WRN] Target culture is missing!


Usage:

  webSystem translate [options]

Options:

--culture|-c <culture>                       Target culture. eg: zh-Hans
--reference-culture|-r <culture>             Default: en
--output|-o <file-name>                      Output file name, Default webSystem-translation.json
--all-values|-all                            Include all keys. Default false
--apply|-a                                   Creates or updates the file for the translated culture.
--file|-f <file-name>                        Default: webSystem-translation.json

Examples:

  webSystem translate -c zh-Hans
  webSystem translate -c zh-Hans -r en -a
  webSystem translate --apply
  webSystem translate -a -f my-translation.json

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.webSystem.io/en/webSystem/latest/CLI

[11:55:34 INF] WEBSYSTEM CLI (https://webSystem.io)
[11:55:34 INF] Version 3.1.0-dev (Development channel)
[11:55:34 WRN] No solution or project found in this directory.


Usage:

  webSystem update [options]

Options:
-p|--include-previews                       (if supported by the template)
--npm                                       (Only updates NPM packages)
--nuget                                     (Only updates Nuget packages)
-sp|--solution-path                         (Specify the solution path)
-sn|--solution-name                         (Specify the solution name)
--check-all                                 (Check the new version of each package separately)

Some examples:

  webSystem update
  webSystem update -p
  webSystem update -sp "D:\projects\" -sn Acme.BookStore

See the documentation for more info: https://docs.webSystem.io/en/webSystem/latest/CLI
